channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Location sharing is the latest way to hard launch a relationship
item.description: Are you location sharing official? Whether it’s for practical reasons, like checking if your partner can swing past your favorite takeaway on the way home, or seeing if they have got home safely from a night out, location sharing is quickly becoming the digital equivalent of leaving a toothbrush at
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Disney+ just got a dash of ESPN
item.description: Disney. Pixar. Marvel. Star Wars. National Geographic. Hulu. The Walt Disney Co. isn’t a portfolio of brands so much as a portfolio of portfolios of brands. Today it’s expanding its Disney+ streaming service by adding content from yet another of its marquee names: ESPN.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: The FBI is urging telecom firms to boost network security following China’s cyberespionage
item.description: Federal authorities on Tuesday urged telecommunication companies to boost network security following a sprawling Chinese hacking campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra predicted colors: every rumored shade
item.description: We're expecting the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra to be sold in seven shades, and we have a good idea of what they'll be.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Report: AI expands the IT workforce as tech jobs return to offices
item.description: Artificial intelligence is driving growth for the IT workforce, and jobs in the technology sector are stabilizing as the market continues to show moderate growth amidst economic volatility, rapid hiring cycles, and public layoffs, according to a new study by IT staffing firm Motion Recruitment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black zip codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Malicious Play Store apps put 8 million Android users at risk
item.description: It's easy to download malicious apps if you're not careful, and a new report reveals more than a dozen malicious apps with SpyLoan malware have been available at the Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The FBI now says encryption is good for you
item.description: Apple has faced an unequal battle in recent years as some lawmakers, the FBI, and regulators insist that the company create backdoors through which to access messages and other parts of its platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on iPhone
item.description: Tech wizard Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to delete old unwanted texts and spam messages from cluttered inboxes of an iPhone in bulk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The UAE’s digital transformation: A visionary leap into the future
item.description: As the United Arab Emirates celebrates its 53rd National Day, it reflects on an extraordinary journey of transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stanford misinformation expert admits his chatbot use led to misinformation in sworn federal court filing
item.description: The professor's legal imbroglio illustrates one of the most common problems with an AI technology that has taken the world by storm.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: China responds to U.S chip sanctions with a ban on these key materials
item.description: China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony, and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Arista Networks Enhances Campus Networking Portfolio with Switch Aggregation Features
item.description: Arista Networks introduces new features to its campus networking portfolio, enabling enterprise customers to group and manage switches more efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How AI agents will transform the future of work
item.description: At first, robotic process automation coupled with low-code platforms and orchestration tools propelled many organizations to increase productivity and scale business operations. Virtual agents and chatbots then took automation one step further by enabling a conversational experience.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Linux fundamentals: Viewing files, commands, processes and systems
item.description: Linux provides a useful array of commands for managing file permissions, getting information on commands, displaying running processes and displaying details on the system itself.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bipartisan bill would study AI's impact on banking, housing
item.description: A new bill with bipartisan support would commission studies on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the banking and housing sectors, where lawmakers have been sounding an alarm about algorithmic price fixing and commercial misconduct enabled by new technologies.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: It looks like Apple’s forthcoming M5 chips are all about AI
item.description: What processors will be running the Apple bargains we’ll be seeking to purchase on Cyber Monday 2025? Presumably, Apple’s M5 chips will be inside some of them, with work already under way on the next amazing Apple Silicon processor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS brings RAG evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge feature to Amazon Bedrock
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has updated Amazon Bedrock with features designed to help enterprises streamline the testing of applications before deployment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS’ Amazon Connect service gets new-gen AI updates
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has updated its Amazon Connect contact center service to help enterprises offer a better end-to-end customer experience, the company announced at its ongoing annual re:Invent conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bitcoin flirts with $100K as crypto industry rallies for Trump
item.description: Bitcoin has blasted through a series of record highs since President-elect Trump’s election victory as the crypto industry anticipates a significant shift in federal policy.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill
